title: Cam20 minute version outrages Japanese authorities
display_title: Cam20 minute version outrages Japanese authorities
wikitext:
[]: Cam(20 minute version) outrages Japanese authorities             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:38 pm http://www.cartoonleap.com/2009/07/06/evangelion-2-0-cam-version-outrage-japanese-authorities/ I felt the need to post this because to some this could be big new. Some are hoping for a cam recording(or a leak just period), but with this, it seems that any hope for one has been shortened. If this didn't warrant its own thread, I'm sorry, but I didn't know where else to put it.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:43 pm heh, what did they expect? a film in that many theaters, that popular, yet you couldn't justify having someone there at each of them to check or to have high-tech sensor equipment

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:46 pm they could also do what we do and have simultaneous movie launches, but nooooooo

[]:             Quote Post by OKdesuKA » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:51 pm Seriously, I have seen worst flicks air at theatres and I know there are a good amount of EVA fans out there that would watch the Rebuild's if they played in theatre. Well that is if they don't use dub since it is very clear you all hate it lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:54 pm OKdesuKA wrote: Well that is if they don't use dub since it is very clear you all hate it lol.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:54 pm Actually, I've heard nothing but good news about the new dub. Honestly, I'd watch either one. After all, the story and art is the most important thing.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jul 08, 2009 2:09 pm Just read the original sauce for that news, on Yahoo.co.jp, and I failed to read anything about authorities commenting on anything. It only says that before "Jyo" was screened, certain law was passed on Japan with up to 10 years in prision an 10*10000 yen in fines for people who do the screeners. Still, read no official comments, as in authorities or the Khara people or any of the other copyright holders.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jul 08, 2009 2:34 pm backseatjesus wrote: Actually, I've heard nothing but good news about the new dub. Honestly, I'd watch either one. After all, the story and art is the most important thing.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Wed Jul 08, 2009 2:40 pm NAveryW wrote: backseatjesus wrote: Actually, I've heard nothing but good news about the new dub. Honestly, I'd watch either one. After all, the story and art is the most important thing.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Jul 08, 2009 2:55 pm backseatjesus wrote: NAveryW wrote: backseatjesus wrote: Actually, I've heard nothing but good news about the new dub. Honestly, I'd watch either one. After all, the story and art is the most important thing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:10 pm Reichu wrote: OKdesuKA wrote: Well that is if they don't use dub since it is very clear you all hate it lol.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:17 pm I made a mistake. I shouldn't have acknowledged someone's ignorance. Guys, please keep the Dub vs. Sub crap out of this thread. That issue isn't important right now. On to the real issue, do you think all this crap will make security at the film a lot more "secure"?

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:21 pm backseatjesus wrote: On to the real issue, do you think all this crap will make security at the film a lot more "secure"?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:35 pm Axell wrote: simultaneous movie launches

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:38 pm Looks like another long wait. :/ I wouldn't mind watching a camrip of the quality found in Rebuild 1's camrip. It's kind of nice, watching it with the camrip and then the high-quality version later.

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:38 pm The visuals for this CAM version are just terrible, off centered and badly framed but people don’t care for that anyway as they are pretty desperate and impatient to watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jul 08, 2009 3:50 pm The Jyo camrip was leaked by someone at Khara, or some insider. It wasn't done a-la clandestine, but more of an arranged way... (still camera in good position, never moving throughout the film, etc...). This time, all the camrips were made by simple viewers.

[]:             Quote Post by Tab » Wed Jul 08, 2009 4:09 pm There is already a thread about the cam and a project set up to translate and make it watchable . We don't host the cam at the moment so Japanese authorities can f<THIS COMMENT HAS BEEN EDITED DUE TO A COPYRIGHT CLAIM BY STUDIO KHARA>

[]: author of the post             Quote Post by toonleap » Wed Jul 08, 2009 4:21 pm I Accidentally Dracula wrote: The visuals for this CAM version are just terrible, off centered and badly framed but people don’t care for that anyway as they are pretty desperate and impatient to watch it.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Wed Jul 08, 2009 4:59 pm I guess this means that now its Americans that are three years ahead of the curve? You remember the shit people went through for press screenings of Star Wars 3? Seriously, I can actually imagine that the concept of CAMrip could hurt Japanese culture. You think of all the crazy hikkimori with their betamax/vhs/laserdisc/dvd/bluray collections... Its like heaven for content producers. Its like the Japanese have a genetic predisposition to pay and consume. Its awesome.
